name: SQL Injection
description: Improper neutralization of special elements in SQL commands. Attacker can manipulate database queries.
remediation: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements. Never concatenate user input into SQL strings.

name: Hardcoded Credentials
description: Software contains hardcoded credentials such as passwords, API keys, or tokens.
remediation: Store credentials in environment variables or a secrets manager. Never hardcode sensitive values.

name: OS Command Injection
description: Improper neutralization of special elements in OS commands.
remediation: Avoid shell=True in subprocess calls. Use list arguments and validate all inputs.

name: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
description: Use of broken or weak cryptographic algorithms like MD5 or SHA1 for passwords.
remediation: Use bcrypt, argon2, or SHA-256+ for password hashing.

name: Deserialization of Untrusted Data
description: Deserializing untrusted data can lead to remote code execution.
remediation: Avoid pickle/yaml.load with untrusted data. Use json or safe loaders.

name: Path Traversal
description: Improper limitation of a pathname to a restricted directory.
remediation: Validate and sanitize file paths. Use os.path.realpath and check against allowed base directories.

name: Weak PRNG
description: Use of cryptographically weak pseudo-random number generator.
remediation: Use secrets module or os.urandom() for security-sensitive random values.

name: Open Redirect
remediation: Validate redirect URLs against an allowlist of trusted domains.
